content.text:
Information Systems Architecture
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEF21  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Understand the fundamental concepts of information systems architecture
Analyse and design IS architectures for various contexts
Learn about the technologies and tools used in architecture design and implementation
Evaluate and improve information system performance
Recommended prior knowledge:
Information systems.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to IS Architecture
Definition and role of information systems: role in the enterprise, evolution of IS
IS architecture: definition and key components (hardware, software, databases, networks)
Architecture types: centralised, distributed, client-server, N-tier, peer-to-peer, service-oriented, microservices, cloud computing
Relationship between IS Architecture and IS Urbanisation
Challenges and issues in IS architecture within enterprise strategy
Architecture Design and Modelling
Models and notations (UML, SysML, BPMN)
Business process architecture
Data architecture
Application and services architecture
Technical and infrastructure architecture
Interaction models
Modelling and engineering tools (Enterprise Architect, Sparx Systems, Open Agile Architecture, etc.)
Architecture Governance and Evolution
IS life cycle management: design, development, deployment, maintenance, obsolescence
Enterprise architecture reference frameworks: TOGAF, Zachman, maturity models
Architecture evolution: trends, impact of new technologies (AI, Blockchain, IoT)
Architecture Evaluation and Optimisation
IS quality (performance, security, scalability, etc.)
Evaluation methods and tools
Performance and scalability optimisation
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
TOGAF (2009).
The Open Group Architecture Framework
. The Open Group.
Zachman, John A. (1987). A Framework of Information Systems Architecture.
IBM Systems Journal
, Vol. 26, no. 3.
Thomas Erl, Zaigham Mahmood, Ricardo Puttini (2013).
Cloud Computing: Concepts, Technology & Architecture
. Pearson Education.
Jeanne W. Ross, Peter Weill, David Robertson (2006).
Enterprise Architecture as Strategy
.
